metadata.name: Bronze
metadata.title: Bronze Laser Cleaning Settings
metadata.description: Detailed machine settings, parameter relationships, diagnostic procedures, and troubleshooting guides for optimizing bronze laser cleaning operations.

author.name: Yi-Chun Lin
author.title: Ph.D.
credentials: Ph.D. Materials Engineering, National Taiwan University, 2018
Post-Ph.D. fellowship at TSMC's laser fab lab, 2018-2020
Assisted in projects on ultrafast laser applications
author.imageAlt: Yi-Chun Lin, Ph.D., Laser Processing Engineer at National Taiwan University, in lab setting

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Bronze oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength with good absorption in Bronze oxides and moderate substrate absorption

spotSize.description: Optimal spot diameter for balancing cleaning efficiency and processing speed

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning coverage while maintaining pulse energy

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective Bronze oxide removal while preserving substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient oxide layer ablation with minimal thermal diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform oxide removal without substrate damage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete oxide removal with safety margin

overlapRatio.description: Optimal pulse overlap for uniform cleaning coverage without excessive thermal buildup
